message:
perf(types): implement lazy-clone owned/shared optimization for PageData and SmallText

PageData previously used Arc<[u8]> unconditionally, paying refcount
overhead even for pages that are created, used, and discarded within a
single owner's lifetime. SmallText had the same issue with heap strings.

PageData optimization:
- Replace bare Arc<[u8]> with Owned { bytes: Vec<u8>, shared: OnceLock<Arc<[u8]>> }
  / Shared(Arc<[u8]>) enum
- Fresh pages stay on Vec (no refcount) until first clone lazily
  promotes to Arc via OnceLock
- as_bytes_mut() on owned pages avoids Arc::make_mut
- into_vec() on owned pages avoids allocation + copy
- Tests verify lazy promotion semantics and mutation isolation

SmallText optimization:
- Heap strings become HeapOwned { text: Box<str>, shared: OnceLock<Arc<str>> }
  until first clone promotes to HeapShared(Arc<str>)
- Custom Clone impls for SmallText and SmallTextRepr that trigger
  lazy promotion only when needed
- Test verifies lazy promotion behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
